suggestions.reason: The assertion only checks that a rating element exists, not that it correctly renders exactly `rating` filled stars out of 5. A component that always shows 5 filled stars would also pass. Consider asserting the rating prop constrains visual output (e.g., conditional styling based on the rating value).

No assertion checks that the showcase page (or any consumer) actually imports from the new directory-based path. The component could be correctly structured but never wired up. The showcase page output shows this was done correctly, but it goes unchecked.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover structural conventions well. The main gap is that UI-behavior assertions (rating rendering, avatar fallback logic) only check presence, not correctness.
